reason: Unprovenanced analysis fact: the crux inference 'each partial sum is real-linear ... and their norm-limit R is real-linear' silently uses that an operator-norm limit of real-linear maps is real-linear (equivalently, continuity of vector-space operations / closedness of the space of linear maps under norm limits). This bare analysis fact has NO refs/ provenance anywhere in the workspace. Node 1.2 only establishes that the partial sums sum_{k<=N} C(-1/2,k) U^k converge to R in norm; it says nothing about linearity being preserved in the limit. Per the project rule that there are no 'standard facts', the limit-of-linear-is-linear step must be derived from a cited refs/ source or prior node. As stated it is a gap (and it is the only place the partial-sum-to-R passage actually does any work for linearity of R).
